meaning: vt.给予选举权,给予自治权,解放
examples: The company voted to enfranchise its 120 women members.
New Zealand was the first country enfranchise women . It gave them the vote in 1895 .
But - and then politically enfranchise the Sunnis for sure - reconciliation, amnesty, has to be the centerpiece.
The proposal, aired at a City council hearing Thursday, would mark the biggest expansion yet of efforts to enfranchise immigrants.
